attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Wash the coffee mug and place it in the coffee maker
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene is a kitchen counter area with several objects relevant to the task of washing a coffee mug and placing it in a coffee maker.

**Objects and Their Types:**

- **Receptacles:**
    - **Sink:** A stainless steel sink basin with a faucet. It is a receptacle capable of holding objects for cleaning. It is located on the right side of the counter, under a window. Inside the sink, there is a dirty glass, a potato, a dirty bowl, and a knife.
    - **Coffee Maker:** A blue, boxy appliance with a spout and a drip tray. It is a receptacle designed to hold a mug for brewing. It is located on the left side of the counter, next to a red toaster. A white coffee mug is currently sitting on its drip tray.
    - **Toaster:** A red, two-slot toaster. It is not a receptacle in the context of the domain as it does not hold items for the purpose of the defined actions.

- **Objects:**
    - **Coffee Mug:** A white ceramic mug with a handle. It is currently placed on the drip tray of the coffee maker. It is visibly dirty, with brown stains inside.
    - **Wine Bottle:** A dark green glass bottle with a label, lying on its side on the counter to the left of the coffee maker.
    - **Fork:** A metal fork, lying on the counter next to the wine bottle.
    - **Knife:** A metal knife with a yellow handle, located inside the sink.
    - **Glass:** A clear drinking glass, inside the sink, partially filled with water and residue.
    - **Potato:** A brown potato, inside the sink.
    - **Bowl:** A grey, speckled bowl, inside the sink.
    - **Cabinets:** White cabinets with silver handles are located below the counter. The sink is installed in the countertop above these cabinets.

**Quantities:**

- Receptacles: 2 (Sink, Coffee Maker)
- Objects: 7 (Coffee Mug, Wine Bottle, Fork, Knife, Glass, Potato, Bowl)

**Spatial Relationships:**

- The **Coffee Mug** is currently **inReceptacle** the **Coffee Maker** (specifically on its drip tray).
- The **Knife**, **Glass**, **Potato**, and **Bowl** are all **inReceptacle** the **Sink**.
- The **Wine Bottle** and **Fork** are on the countertop, to the left of the coffee maker.
- The **Coffee Maker** is to the left of the **Toaster**.
- The **Sink** is to the right of the **Coffee Maker**.
- The **Cabinets** are directly below the **Sink** and the **Coffee Maker**.

**Current State of Objects Relevant to the Task:**

- The **Coffee Mug** is dirty and needs to be cleaned. It is currently on the coffee maker, but the task requires it to be washed first.
- The **Sink** is available for cleaning and is not currently in use for the cleaning action.
- The **Coffee Maker** is not currently turned on, as there is no indication of it being in operation (e.g., no steam, no light).

**Visual Properties:**

- The **Coffee Maker** is a solid blue color with a black control panel and a silver spout.
- The **Coffee Mug** is white with visible brown coffee stains inside.
- The **Sink** is stainless steel with a modern, curved faucet.
- The **Toaster** is a deep red color.
- The **Cabinets** are white with flat-panel doors and silver, horizontal handles.
- The countertop is a speckled grey and white granite.
- The floor is a light wood laminate.
- The window above the sink has a white frame and lets in natural light, casting shadows on the countertop.

The robot's task is to first move to the coffee maker, pick up the dirty mug, then move to the sink, clean the mug, and finally return to the coffee maker to place the clean mug back on it.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem wash_mug_and_place_in_coffee_maker)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        coffee_maker - receptacle
        sink - receptacle
        coffee_mug - object
        wine_bottle - object
        fork - object
        knife - object
        glass - object
        potato - object
        bowl - object
        toaster - object
        cabinets - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Initial locations
        (atLocation robot coffee_maker) ; Robot starts near the coffee maker
        ; Objects in receptacles
        (inReceptacle coffee_mug coffee_maker)
        (inReceptacle knife sink)
        (inReceptacle glass sink)
        (inReceptacle potato sink)
        (inReceptacle bowl sink)
        ; Object properties
        (not (isClean coffee_mug))
        (not (isHot coffee_mug))
        (not (isCool coffee_mug))
        (not (isSliced coffee_mug))
        (not (isOn coffee_maker))
        (not (isToggled coffee_maker))
        ; Receptacle properties
        (not (opened sink)) ; Sink is not openable in this domain, so this is irrelevant but included for completeness
        (not (opened coffee_maker)) ; Coffee maker is not openable in this domain
        ; Agent state
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle coffee_mug coffee_maker)
            (isClean coffee_mug)
        )
    )
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem wash_mug_and_place_in_coffee_maker)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        coffee_maker - receptacle
        sink - receptacle
        coffee_mug - object
        wine_bottle - object
        fork - object
        knife - object
        glass - object
        potato - object
        bowl - object
        toaster - object
        cabinets - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Initial locations
        (atLocation robot coffee_maker) ; Robot starts near the coffee maker
        ; Objects in receptacles
        (inReceptacle coffee_mug coffee_maker)
        (inReceptacle knife sink)
        (inReceptacle glass sink)
        (inReceptacle potato sink)
        (inReceptacle bowl sink)
        ; Object properties
        (not (isClean coffee_mug))
        (not (isHot coffee_mug))
        (not (isCool coffee_mug))
        (not (isSliced coffee_mug))
        (not (isOn coffee_maker))
        (not (isToggled coffee_maker))
        ; Receptacle properties
        (not (opened sink)) ; Sink is not openable in this domain, so this is irrelevant but included for completeness
        (not (opened coffee_maker)) ; Coffee maker is not openable in this domain
        ; Agent state
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle coffee_mug coffee_maker)
            (isClean coffee_mug)
        )
    )
)

The scene depicts a kitchen countertop area with several objects relevant to the task. The primary objects of interest are a coffee mug, a coffee maker, and a sink.

1.  **Objects and their types:**
    *   **Coffee Mug (object):** A white ceramic mug with a handle, currently positioned on the drip tray of the blue coffee maker. It is visibly stained, indicating it is not clean.
    *   **Coffee Maker (receptacle):** A large, blue appliance with a silver spout and a control panel featuring a coffee cup icon. It has a drip tray at its base, which functions as a receptacle holding the coffee mug. The coffee maker is situated on the speckled countertop.
    *   **Sink (receptacle):** A stainless steel sink basin, which is a receptacle. It is embedded in the countertop and contains a few dirty items: a clear glass, a small brown potato, and a metal bowl. A faucet is positioned above it.
    *   **Toaster (object):** A red, two-slice toaster is located to the right of the coffee maker on the countertop.
    *   **Wine Bottle (object):** A dark green wine bottle is lying on its side on the countertop to the left of the coffee maker.
    *   **Fork (object):** A metal fork is lying on the countertop near the wine bottle.
    *   **Cabinets (receptacle):** White cabinets with silver handles are visible below the countertop. These are openable receptacles.
    *   **Knife (object):** A knife with a yellow handle is visible inside the sink, resting against the side.

2.  **Quantities:**
    *   There is one coffee mug.
    *   There is one coffee maker.
    *   There is one sink.
    *   There is one toaster.
    *   There is one wine bottle.
    *   There is one fork.
    *   There is one knife.
    *   There are multiple cabinets.

3.  **Spatial Relationships:**
    *   The coffee mug is currently **in** the coffee maker's drip tray, which acts as a receptacle. (`inReceptacle coffee_mug coffee_maker`)
    *   The coffee maker is on the countertop, to the left of the red toaster.
    *   The sink is located to the left of the coffee maker, under a window.
    *   The knife is inside the sink.
    *   The wine bottle and fork are on the countertop to the left of the coffee maker.
    *   The cabinets are located beneath the countertop and sink.

4.  **Current State of Objects:**
    *   The coffee mug is dirty (not clean) and is currently sitting in the coffee maker.
    *   The coffee maker is not currently active (not `isOn` or `isToggled`).
    *   The sink is filled with dirty dishes, indicating it is ready to be used for cleaning.
    *   The knife is in the sink and is not being held.

5.  **Visual Properties:**
    *   The coffee mug is white with a visible handle and interior stains.
    *   The coffee maker is a prominent blue rectangular appliance with a silver spout and a digital display.
    *   The sink is a standard stainless steel basin with a modern faucet.
    *   The countertop is a light-colored, speckled material, possibly granite or quartz.
    *   The cabinets are white with simple, flat-panel doors and metallic bar handles.
    *   The toaster is a vibrant red color.
    *   The wine bottle is dark green with a label.
    *   The fork is metallic and has four tines.
    *   The knife has a distinct yellow handle and a silver blade.

    :effect (and

                )
            )
 )

The scene depicts a kitchen countertop area with several objects relevant to the task. The primary objects of interest are a coffee mug, a coffee maker, and a sink.

1. Objects and their types:

   - Coffee maker: This is a large blue appliance with a spout and a drip tray. It is not defined as a receptacle in the domain, but it holds the coffee mug on its tray. It has a small display with a coffee cup icon. It is situated on the countertop to the right of a wine bottle and fork, and next to a red toaster.
   - Sink: This is a stainless steel sink with a faucet, and it is classified as a receptacle. It contains a glass, a potato, a bowl, and a knife. The sink is located to the right of the coffee maker and is embedded in the countertop.
   - Knife: This is an object (and also a knife type) and is currently inside the sink, lying next to a glass and a potato.
   - Glass: An object, currently in the sink.
   - Potato: An object, currently in the sink.
   - Bowl: An object, currently in the sink.
   - Wine bottle: An object, lying on its side on the countertop to the left of the coffee maker.
   - Fork: An object, lying on the countertop next to the wine bottle.
   - Toaster: A red appliance on the countertop next to the coffee maker. It is not defined in the domain and is not relevant to the task.

2. Quantities:
   - 1 coffee mug
   - 1 coffee maker
   - 1 sink (receptacle)
   - 1 knife
   - 1 glass
   - 1 potato
   - 1 bowl
   - 1 wine bottle
   - 1 fork
   - 1 toaster

3. Spatial relationships:
   - The coffee mug is on the coffee maker’s drip tray (inReceptacle is not applicable as the coffee maker is not a receptacle in the domain, but visually it is resting on it).
   - The sink contains the glass, potato, bowl, and knife.
   - The coffee maker is to the left of the sink and to the right of the wine bottle and fork.
   - The toaster is to the right of the coffee maker.
   - The countertop is made of speckled granite, and the cabinets below are white with silver handles.
   - The floor is light wood.

4. Current state of objects relevant to the task:
   - The coffee mug is not clean (isClean is false) and is currently on the coffee maker.
   - The sink is not openable and is not toggled; it is a receptacle that can hold objects.
   - The coffee maker is not toggled (isOn is false) and is not a receptacle in the domain.
   - The knife is in the sink and is not held by any agent.
   - No object is currently held by the agent (holdsAny is false).

5. Visual properties:

   - The sink is stainless steel with a modern faucet.
   - The countertop is speckled gray and white granite.
   - The cabinets are white with horizontal silver handles.
   - The floor is light-colored wood laminate.
   - The toaster is red with black slots.
   - The wine bottle is dark green with a label, lying on its side.
   - The fork is silver and lying flat on the countertop.

The robot must first go to the coffee mug, pick it up, then go to the sink, clean it, and finally return it to the coffee maker. The sink is the only receptacle available for cleaning, and the coffee maker is the target location for placing the cleaned mug.

```pddl
(define (problem wash_mug_and_place_in_coffee_maker)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        coffee_mug - object
        coffee_maker - object
        sink - sink
        knife - knife
        glass - object
        potato - object
        bowl - object
        wine_bottle - object
        fork - object
        toaster - object
    )
    (:init
        ; Initial locations: robot is not at any location
        (not (atLocation robot coffee_mug))
        (not (atLocation robot coffee_maker))
        (not (atLocation robot sink))
        (not (atLocation robot knife))
        (not (atLocation robot glass))
        (not (atLocation robot potato))
        (not (atLocation robot bowl))
        (not (atLocation robot wine_bottle))
        (not (atLocation robot fork))
        (not (atLocation robot toaster))

        ; The coffee mug is on the coffee maker (though coffee maker is not a receptacle, we note its position for planning)
        ; We'll model the mug as being at the coffee maker's location for the purpose of pickup
        (atLocation coffee_mug coffee_maker)

        ; The sink contains several objects
        (inReceptacle glass sink)
        (inReceptacle potato sink)
        (inReceptacle bowl sink)
        (inReceptacle knife sink)

        ; The coffee maker is not a receptacle, so we don't use inReceptacle for the mug
        ; The mug is not clean
        (not (isClean coffee_mug))

        ; No object is held by the agent
        (not (holdsAny robot))

        ; The sink is not openable (as per domain, sink is a receptacle but not openable)
        (not (openable sink))

        ; The coffee maker is not toggled
        (not (isOn coffee_maker))

        ; The knife is not held
        (not (holds robot knife))

        ; The wine bottle and fork are on the countertop (we don't need to model their location for the task, but we note they are present)
        ; We'll assume they are at the countertop, but since we don't have a countertop object, we don't model their location for the robot's actions
    )
    (:goal (and
        ; The coffee mug must be clean
        (isClean coffee_mug)
        ; The coffee mug must be placed back on the coffee maker
        ; Since the coffee maker is not a receptacle, we cannot use inReceptacle. We must model the mug being at the coffee maker's location.
        (atLocation coffee_mug coffee_maker)
    ))
)
```

    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.

    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )

    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject

    :precondition (and
